settings.title: We Assess Your Foundation’s Elevation Levels

elements.htmlCache: We Assess Your Foundation’s Elevation Levels

settings.editor: Super Inspector provides a thorough evaluation of the structural systems that protect your home’s stability, giving you peace of mind. Our inspectors are trained to identify signs of foundation movement caused by expansive soils and changing weather, using advanced tools like Zip Level technology to measure elevation changes and determine whether movement is within normal tolerances or indicates a potential problem.

Home Inspection FAQs

settings.title: Frequently Asked Questions About Texas Foundation Inspections

elements.htmlCache: Frequently Asked Questions About Texas Foundation Inspections

settings.editor: Buying or building a home comes with important decisions. Below are direct answers to common questions about Texas home inspections, what is included, timelines, and what to expect.

elements.htmlCache: Buying or building a home comes with important decisions. Below are direct answers to common questions about Texas home inspections, what is included, timelines, and what to expect.
View All Inspection FAQs

image.alt: A house window at night shows a thermal image: one pane glows red-orange labeled High Heat Loss, while the other glows blue-purple labeled Optimal Insulation. Neon outlines and a color scale highlight temperature differences.
A gray quadcopter drone flies above a tiled rooftop during sunset, with trees and a chimney visible in the blurred background.

ekit_accordion_items.acc_title: What does a foundation inspection include?
ekit_accordion_items.acc_content: We evaluate structural cracking, elevation changes, drainage conditions, slab or pier systems, and signs of movement that may impact your home’s integrity.

ekit_accordion_items.acc_title: How long does a foundation inspection take?
ekit_accordion_items.acc_content: Most inspections take 1–2 hours depending on property size and accessibility.
In most real estate transactions, the buyer covers the inspection, but this can be negotiated.

ekit_accordion_items.acc_title: Should I get a foundation inspection before selling?
ekit_accordion_items.acc_content: Yes. Identifying issues early helps prevent surprises during negotiations.

elements.htmlCache:
What does a foundation inspection include?

We evaluate structural cracking, elevation changes, drainage conditions, slab or pier systems, and signs of movement that may impact your home’s integrity.

How long does a foundation inspection take?

Most inspections take 1–2 hours depending on property size and accessibility.

Who pays for the inspection?

In most real estate transactions, the buyer covers the inspection, but this can be negotiated.

Should I get a foundation inspection before selling?

Yes. Identifying issues early helps prevent surprises during negotiations.

About Us
Home Inspection Frequently Asked Questions

ekit_accordion_items.acc_title: Do I get a copy of my home inspection report?
ekit_accordion_items.acc_content: Absolutely! We will provide a highly detailed report to you when the home inspection is complete. A full digital summary report will be emailed directly to you within 24 hours of the inspection. To see the type of report you can expect, view our sample residential inspection reports.

ekit_accordion_items.acc_title: How long does a home inspection take?
ekit_accordion_items.acc_content: The home inspection usually takes 2 to 4 hours, depending on the size of the house.

ekit_accordion_items.acc_title: How much does your home inspection cost?
ekit_accordion_items.acc_content: Prices are based on the square footage, age of the house, and type of systems in the house. We offer a range of inspection pricing packages designed to fit the property and type of buyer you are.

ekit_accordion_items.acc_title: What does a typical home inspection cover?
ekit_accordion_items.acc_content: Our inspectors will assess the major systems of the home including structural, mechanical, electrical, plumbing, appliances, and various optional systems. These building inspections must be done in compliance with the Standards of Practice as directed by the Texas Real Estate Commission.

ekit_accordion_items.acc_title: Can I attend the home inspection and ask questions?
ekit_accordion_items.acc_content: You certainly can! Clients are encouraged to attend the inspection and ask questions so the conditions reported are thoroughly understood.

Yes. Our home inspectors are licensed by their regulatory bodies and are kept up to date with industry standards with continuous and frequent trainings to keep them the best home inspectors. 

Our team also includes licensed HVAC Technicians (TACLB00022053E) and licensed pest management professionals, as regulated by the Texas Department of Agriculture. Our pest management professionals are licensed through our affiliate company, Service 1st Pest and Lawn to inspect for wood destroying insects, including termites and carpenter ants. Wood Destroying Insect inspections must be conducted in compliance with the requirements of the Texas Structural Pest Control Board.

elements.htmlCache:
Do I get a copy of my home inspection report?

Absolutely! We will provide a highly detailed report to you when the home inspection is complete. A full digital summary report will be emailed directly to you within 24 hours of the inspection. To see the type of report you can expect, view our sample residential inspection reports.

How long does a home inspection take?

The home inspection usually takes 2 to 4 hours, depending on the size of the house.

How much does your home inspection cost?

Prices are based on the square footage, age of the house, and type of systems in the house. We offer a range of inspection pricing packages designed to fit the property and type of buyer you are.

What does a typical home inspection cover?

Our inspectors will assess the major systems of the home including structural, mechanical, electrical, plumbing, appliances, and various optional systems. These building inspections must be done in compliance with the Standards of Practice as directed by the Texas Real Estate Commission.

Can I attend the home inspection and ask questions?

You certainly can! Clients are encouraged to attend the inspection and ask questions so the conditions reported are thoroughly understood.

Are your home inspectors qualified?

Yes. Our home inspectors are licensed by their regulatory bodies and are kept up to date with industry standards with continuous and frequent trainings to keep them the best home inspectors. 

Our team also includes licensed HVAC Technicians (TACLB00022053E) and licensed pest management professionals, as regulated by the Texas Department of Agriculture. Our pest management professionals are licensed through our affiliate company, Service 1st Pest and Lawn to inspect for wood destroying insects, including termites and carpenter ants. Wood Destroying Insect inspections must be conducted in compliance with the requirements of the Texas Structural Pest Control Board.
